definition: TWD and KRW are decoupling from traditional EM FX factor models toward semiconductor cycle proxy status. TWD/KRW correlation with SOX index rising from ~0.3 to ~0.7; R-squared against standard EM factors (VIX, EMBI spread, DXY) declining. TSMC AI/HPC revenue rose from ~10% to ~50% of total. Taiwan current account surplus ~14% of GDP. Geopolitical tail risk is qualitatively different from standard EM political risk (discontinuous, not gradual).
evidence: TWD/KRW correlation with SOX rising from ~0.3 to ~0.7
R-squared against standard EM factors declining
TSMC AI/HPC revenue from ~10% to ~50% of total
Taiwan current account surplus ~14% of GDP
open_questions: Should TWD/KRW be reclassified out of EM benchmarks for portfolio construction purposes?
How does the discontinuous geopolitical tail risk (Taiwan Strait) interact with the semiconductor proxy status?
Does the decoupling create mispricing in traditional EM benchmarks that mix TWD/KRW with BRL/ZAR/TRY?
